markdown.markdown:
```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(ISOweek) library(leaflet) library(zoo) library(forecast) library(spdep) library(sf) library(councildown) library(classInt) ``` ```{r} noise <- read.csv("../Data/311_Service_Requests.csv") # List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) # Keep only social noise noise <- noise %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. %in% c("Loud Music/Party", "Loud Talking", "Car/Truck Music", "Noise, Ice Cream Truck (NR4)")) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # Graph Complaints (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) ```{r} ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() ggplot(noise_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "4 months", date_labels = "%Y-%m") + labs(title = "Monthly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Month", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Highly seasonal with peaks in June and September, troughs in January. # Break it down by complaint type to see where this is coming from. ggplot(noise_yearly, aes(x = year, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Yearly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Year", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Identify week-level anomalies (rolling z-score) noise_weekly <- noise_weekly %>% arrange(year, week) %>% mutate( roll_mean = rollmean(total_complaints, 8, fill = NA, align = "right"), roll_sd = rollapply(total_complaints, 8, sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), z = (total_complaints - roll_mean) / roll_sd, spike = z > 2, date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-1")) ) ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = date, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), linetype = "dashed") + geom_point(data = subset(noise_weekly, spike), color = "red") + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts with Spikes Highlighted", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Just one hotspot at 6-week intervals # Using an 8-week average, 11 hotspots were identified, all throughout the year. May be more sensitive to hotspots since the pattern is so simple. ``` # Monthly seasonality tests for all types ```{r} noise_monthly_type <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") count(noise_ll, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% mutate(`%` = round(n/sum(n) * 100)) %>% arrange(desc(n)) ggplot(noise_monthly_type, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., scales = "free_y") + labs(title = "Monthly Noise Complaints by Type", x = "Month", y = "Total Complaints") + theme_minimal() # This category is dominated by Loud Music/Party, with Ice Cream Truck being a rarity. # All social noise categories have summer seasonality. The June/September peak style appears almost universally, with ice cream truck complaints retaining a more singular peak structure in June. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model does not have a specific term for seasonality, but covers it with 52-week differencing (0,1,0)[52]. # Suggests a very clean seasonal component: a more precise seasonal term could be added, but the complication outweighs the marginal change to AIC. # Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern. decomp <- stl(noise_ts, s.window = "periodic") plot(decomp) # Clear seasonal pattern. # Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints fit_naive <- naive(noise_ts) fit_tbats <- tbats(noise_ts) # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE # e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) # }) # # e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(naive(y), h = h) # }) # # e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(tbats(y), h = h) # }) # # sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model remains the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} noise_sf <- st_as_sf(noise_ll, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) # Read in demographics data from councilcount, replace likely population flukes with 0. ntas <- st_read("../Data/nta-demo.geojson") %>% st_transform(st_crs(noise_sf)) %>% select(nta, total_popE, geometry) %>% mutate(total_popE = if_else(total_popE < 100, 0, total_popE)) noise_with_nta <- st_join(noise_sf, ntas, join = st_within) nta_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") nta_map <- ntas %>% left_join(nta_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000)) jenks_breaks <- classIntervals(nta_map$total_complaints[nta_map$total_complaints > 0], n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Adjust slightly to put the two outliers (>16k) in their own color jenks_breaks[5] = 10047 warm_pal_jenks <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = nta_map$total_complaints, bins = jenks_breaks, na.color = "transparent") nta_map$fill <- ifelse(nta_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_jenks(nta_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Total complaints are highest in Washington Heights. # There's one hotspot in Upper Manhattan and bordering parts of The Bronx, plus another in North-Central Brooklyn. ``` # Per capita calculations ```{r} # Map complaints per capita jenks_breaks_pc <- classIntervals( round(nta_map$complaints_pc[nta_map$total_popE > 0]), n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Tweak again to separate the upper echelon (and include #1) jenks_breaks_pc[5] = 165 warm_pal_jenks_pc <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = round(nta_map$complaints_pc), bins = jenks_breaks_pc, na.color = "grey" ) nta_map$fill_pc <- case_when( nta_map$total_popE == 0 ~ "grey", # no population → gray TRUE ~ warm_pal_jenks_pc(round(nta_map$complaints_pc)) ) # Belt of complaints stays strong per capita from West Harlem to Inwood. # Bronx and North-Central Brooklyn remain elevated. Midtown-Soho and near-ferry Staten Island arise with higher complaints per capita. ``` # Gi* Calculations ```{r} # Collapse complaints with duplicate locations for cluster analysis noise_collapsed <- noise_sf %>% group_by(Location) %>% summarise(total_obs = n(), .groups = "drop") # Project for distance-based neighbors (meters) noise_collapsed_proj <- st_transform(noise_collapsed, 32618) # UTM zone 18N for NYC coords <- st_coordinates(noise_collapsed_proj) # Define neighbors within 500 m neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500) weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) gi_star <- localG(noise_collapsed_proj$total_obs, listw = weights, zero.policy = TRUE) noise_collapsed_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star) noise_collapsed_leaflet <- st_transform(noise_collapsed_proj, 4326) # back to WGS84 noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 2.58) # "Narrows down" to 18k coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 1% level) jenks_breaks_gi <- classIntervals( round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), n = 3, style = "jenks")$brks pal <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("cool"), domain = round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), bins = jenks_breaks_gi, na.color = "grey" ) # Upper Manhattan and the nearby Bronx form a sea of social noise hotspots. It's mostly contiguous, with enclaves in West Farms and Soundview. # Other clusters include Yorkville, Midtown, Tribeca/Soho, Williamsburg, Bushwick, Flatbush/PLG/Crown Heights, Madison (South Brooklyn), and Stapleton (Staten Island). ``` # Main Visual ```{r} leaflet() %>% # Basemap addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # NTA CHOROPLETH (BASE LAYER) # ------------------------------------------------- addPolygons( data = nta_map, group = "NTA Complaints per Capita", fillColor = ~fill_pc, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints per 1k Residents: ", round(complaints_pc, 1) ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # GI* HOTSPOT POINTS (OVERLAY) # ------------------------------------------------- addCircleMarkers( data = noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers, group = "Complaint Hotspots", radius = 5, fillColor = ~pal(GiZ), fillOpacity = 0.8, stroke = TRUE, weight = 1, color = "blue", popup = ~paste0( "Gi* Z-score: ", round(GiZ, 2), "
", "Total Complaints: ", total_obs ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LEGENDS # ------------------------------------------------- addLegend( position = "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks_pc, values = nta_map$complaints_pc, title = "Complaints per 1,000 Residents", opacity = 0.7 ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LAYER CONTROL # ------------------------------------------------- addLayersControl( overlayGroups = c("Complaint Hotspots"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) %>% # Default view setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) ```

```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(ISOweek) library(leaflet) library(zoo) library(forecast) library(spdep) library(sf) library(councildown) library(classInt) ``` # Load and clean data ```{r} noise <- read.csv("../Data/311_Service_Requests.csv") # List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) # Keep only social noise noise <- noise %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. %in% c("Loud Music/Party", "Loud Talking", "Car/Truck Music", "Noise, Ice Cream Truck (NR4)")) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # Graph Complaints (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) ```{r} ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() ggplot(noise_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "4 months", date_labels = "%Y-%m") + labs(title = "Monthly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Month", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Just one hotspot at 6-week intervals # Using an 8-week average, 11 hotspots were identified, all throughout the year. May be more sensitive to hotspots since the pattern is so simple. ``` # Monthly seasonality tests for all types ```{r} noise_monthly_type <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") count(noise_ll, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% mutate(`%` = round(n/sum(n) * 100)) %>% arrange(desc(n)) ggplot(noise_monthly_type, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., scales = "free_y") + labs(title = "Monthly Noise Complaints by Type", x = "Month", y = "Total Complaints") + theme_minimal() # This category is dominated by Loud Music/Party, with Ice Cream Truck being a rarity. # All social noise categories have summer seasonality. The June/September peak style appears almost universally, with ice cream truck complaints retaining a more singular peak structure in June. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model does not have a specific term for seasonality, but covers it with 52-week differencing (0,1,0)[52]. # Suggests a very clean seasonal component: a more precise seasonal term could be added, but the complication outweighs the marginal change to AIC. # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE # e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) # }) # # e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(naive(y), h = h) # }) # # e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(tbats(y), h = h) # }) # # sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model remains the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} noise_sf <- st_as_sf(noise_ll, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) # Adjust slightly to put the two outliers (>16k) in their own color jenks_breaks[5] = 10047 warm_pal_jenks <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = nta_map$total_complaints, bins = jenks_breaks, na.color = "transparent") nta_map$fill <- ifelse(nta_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_jenks(nta_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Total complaints are highest in Washington Heights. # There's one hotspot in Upper Manhattan and bordering parts of The Bronx, plus another in North-Central Brooklyn. ``` # Per capita calculations ```{r} # Map complaints per capita jenks_breaks_pc <- classIntervals( round(nta_map$complaints_pc[nta_map$total_popE > 0]), n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Tweak again to separate the upper echelon (and include #1) jenks_breaks_pc[5] = 165 warm_pal_jenks_pc <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = round(nta_map$complaints_pc), bins = jenks_breaks_pc, na.color = "grey" ) nta_map$fill_pc <- case_when( nta_map$total_popE == 0 ~ "grey", # no population → gray TRUE ~ warm_pal_jenks_pc(round(nta_map$complaints_pc)) ) # Belt of complaints stays strong per capita from West Harlem to Inwood. # Bronx and North-Central Brooklyn remain elevated. Midtown-Soho and near-ferry Staten Island arise with higher complaints per capita. ``` # Gi* Calculations ```{r} # Collapse complaints with duplicate locations for cluster analysis noise_collapsed <- noise_sf %>% group_by(Location) %>% summarise(total_obs = n(), .groups = "drop") # Project for distance-based neighbors (meters) noise_collapsed_proj <- st_transform(noise_collapsed, 32618) # UTM zone 18N for NYC coords <- st_coordinates(noise_collapsed_proj) # Define neighbors within 500 m neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500) weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) gi_star <- localG(noise_collapsed_proj$total_obs, listw = weights, zero.policy = TRUE) noise_collapsed_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star) noise_collapsed_leaflet <- st_transform(noise_collapsed_proj, 4326) # back to WGS84 noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 2.58) # "Narrows down" to 18k coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 1% level) jenks_breaks_gi <- classIntervals( round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), n = 3, style = "jenks")$brks pal <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("cool"), domain = round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), bins = jenks_breaks_gi, na.color = "grey" ) # Upper Manhattan and the nearby Bronx form a sea of social noise hotspots. It's mostly contiguous, with enclaves in West Farms and Soundview. # Other clusters include Yorkville, Midtown, Tribeca/Soho, Williamsburg, Bushwick, Flatbush/PLG/Crown Heights, Madison (South Brooklyn), and Stapleton (Staten Island). ``` # Main Visual ```{r} leaflet() %>% # Basemap addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # NTA CHOROPLETH (BASE LAYER) # ------------------------------------------------- addPolygons( data = nta_map, group = "NTA Complaints per Capita", fillColor = ~fill_pc, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints per 1k Residents: ", round(complaints_pc, 1) ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # GI* HOTSPOT POINTS (OVERLAY) # ------------------------------------------------- addCircleMarkers( data = noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers, group = "Complaint Hotspots", radius = 5, fillColor = ~pal(GiZ), fillOpacity = 0.8, stroke = TRUE, weight = 1, color = "blue", popup = ~paste0( "Gi* Z-score: ", round(GiZ, 2), "
", "Total Complaints: ", total_obs ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LEGENDS # ------------------------------------------------- addLegend( position = "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks_pc, values = nta_map$complaints_pc, title = "Complaints per 1,000 Residents", opacity = 0.7 ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LAYER CONTROL # ------------------------------------------------- addLayersControl( overlayGroups = c("Complaint Hotspots"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) %>% # Default view setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) ```

language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: For attribution, please cite this work as:
